concepts.finding: WGAN uses Earth Mover (Wasserstein-1) distance instead of Jensen-Shannon divergence, providing stable training, meaningful loss curves correlating with sample quality, and eliminating mode collapse.
concepts.implication: Wasserstein-based ranking losses may provide better gradient signals than pointwise or pairwise losses for search retrieval models.
concepts.open_question: Does Wasserstein-based ranking loss outperform standard pairwise (margin-based) ranking loss for search retrieval?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A retrieval model trained with Wasserstein ranking loss will achieve 3%+ higher NDCG@10 than one trained with hinge-based pairwise loss.
